chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الشُّهُورِ الَّتِي تُسْتَحَبُّ فِيهَا الْعُمْرَةُ وَ مَنْ أَحْرَمَ فِي شَهْرٍ وَ أَحَلَّ فِي آخَرَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 208 – The months in which the Umra is recommended, and the one who wears Ihraam in one month and frees in another

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/236)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/236)
text: رَجُلٍ أَحْرَمَ فِي شَهْرٍ وَ أَحَلَّ فِي آخَرَ فَقَالَ يُكْتَبُ لَهُ فِي الَّذِي قَدْ نَوَى أَوْ يُكْتَبُ لَهُ فِي أَفْضَلِهِمَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Bin Al Bakhtary, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding a man who wears Ihraam during a month, and frees during another. So heasws said: ‘It would be Written for him regarding that which he intended, or would be Written for him regarding the superior of the two’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Flafs ibn al-Bakhtariy from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Hajjaj who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that a man who assumes the state of Ihram during a month and comes out of it during the next month, the Imam said, ‘This is counted al-‘Umrah in the month in which he has made his intention or al-‘Umrah in the more virtuous month.’”
